title: Example flux processing
code: # download package, if needed # install.packages("remotes") # remotes::install_github("abbylewis/autochamber")  # load package library(autochamber)

title: Example flux processing
text: package includes two example datasets, data_small_genx data_small_chapada. datasets includes days automated flux chamber data. Columns include: TIMESTAMP: time sample CH4d_ppm: methane concentration (ppm) CO2d_ppm: carbon dioxide concentration (ppm) N2Od_ppm: nitrous oxide concentration (ppm). Note included data_small_chapada (measured) Chamber: ID number chamber sampled given time Manifold_Timer (time seconds output sampling manifold) notice data_small_genx also column Format, data_small_chapada columns location Flux_Status. location example grouping variable may relevant flux processing. data_small_chapada includes data two gas analyzers, differentiated location column. need specified many functions . Conversely, Format Flux_Status needed current flux processing workflow, ignored ultimately removed throughout functions .

title: Example flux processing
text: working months years flux data, file sizes can get huge. may helpful isolate recent raw data real-time visualization quality control. generate_recent_raw() function takes dataset raw flux data outputs trimmed file certain number recent days, may stored quickly visualized.
code: recent_raw_genx <- generate_recent_raw(   data_small_genx,   # For real-time workflows, set 'today' via Sys.Date()   # Here, we are instead just using the most recent data   today = lubridate::as_date(max(data_small_genx$TIMESTAMP)),   days = 2 )  recent_raw_chapada <- generate_recent_raw(   data_small_chapada,   group_cols = "location",   today = lubridate::as_date(max(data_small_chapada$TIMESTAMP)),   days = 2 )

title: Example flux processing
text: Let’s take look recent data. plot_raw_data() plots greenhouse gas data gas analyzer, linear slopes given flux interval shown solid black lines.
code: # Only want ~an hour or two for this figure data_to_plot_genx <- head(recent_raw_genx, 500) # Plot plot_raw_data(   data_to_plot_genx,   cutoff_start = 220,   cutoff_end = 510 ) #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x' data_to_plot_chapada <- head(recent_raw_chapada, 500) # Plot plot_raw_data(   data_to_plot_chapada,   cutoff_start = 180,   cutoff_end = 680,   group_cols = "location" ) #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

title: Example flux processing
text: flux intervals look pretty good! , look like linear slope range data specified. Let’s now use flux intervals calculate slopes gases full dataset using calculate_flux().
code: genx_fluxes <- calculate_flux(data_small_genx,   cutoff_start = 220, # Same as above   cutoff_end = 510 )  chapada_fluxes <- calculate_flux(data_small_chapada,   cutoff_start = 180,   cutoff_end = 680,   group_cols = "location" )

title: Example flux processing
text: take look fluxes, can use function plot_calculated_fluxes(), includes options grouping columns, start end dates, daily aggregation (see documentation).

title: Example flux processing
text: project, created maintenance logs record intervals data flagged potentially also set NA. maintenance logs stored google sheets, can access via links . adapting workflow projects, maintenance log need follow general format. sheet needs set anyone link can view. Column order matter, column name capitalization . Start_time: Start window time flagged End_time: End time Description: Text description period flagged Chambers: chambers apply Analyzer: Type gas analyzer. Options CO2/CH4, N2O, Flag: Short text flag added data. E.g., “Chamber maintenance” Remove: Whether remove data window (y/n) Grouping columns included maintenance log. example, see location Chapada maintenance log.
code: genx_fluxes_cleaned <-   add_maintenance_log(     slopes = genx_fluxes,     gs_url = "http://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1_uk8-335NDJOdVU6OjLcxWx4MamNJeVEbVkSmdb9oRs/edit?gid=0#gid=0"   ) #> ✔ Reading from GenX QAQC log. #> ✔ Range Sheet1.  chapada_fluxes_cleaned <-   add_maintenance_log(     slopes = chapada_fluxes,     gs_url = "https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/103PpjEmjLAQkov9ywjA5KxyJiIP3nEWy7V8gWVZhd1M/edit?gid=0#gid=0",     group_cols = "location"   ) #> ✔ Reading from Chapada_QAQC_log. #> ✔ Range Sheet1.

title: Example flux processing
code: genx_fluxes_qaqced <- filter_fluxes_by_SE(   genx_fluxes_cleaned ) #> [1] CH4: 3 removed (2.7%); CO2: 1 removed (0.9%) #> [2] CH4: 1 removed (0.9%); CO2: 0 removed (0%) #> [3] CH4: 2 removed (1.8%); CO2: 2 removed (1.8%) #> [4] CH4: 1 removed (0.9%); CO2: 2 removed (1.8%) #> [5] CH4: 2 removed (1.8%); CO2: 3 removed (2.8%) #> [6] CH4: 2 removed (1.8%); CO2: 2 removed (1.8%) #> [7] CH4: 2 removed (1.8%); CO2: 3 removed (2.7%) #> [8] CH4: 2 removed (1.8%); CO2: 2 removed (1.8%) #> [9] CH4: 2 removed (1.8%); CO2: 3 removed (2.8%) #> [10] CH4: 3 removed (2.7%); CO2: 0 removed (0%) #> [11] CH4: 2 removed (1.8%); CO2: 2 removed (1.8%) #> [12] CH4: 2 removed (1.8%); CO2: 1 removed (0.9%)  chapada_fluxes_qaqced <- filter_fluxes_by_SE(   chapada_fluxes_cleaned,   group_cols = "location" ) #> [10 | high] CH4: 0 removed (0%); CO2: 0 removed (0%) #> [10 | low] CH4: 0 removed (0%); CO2: 3 removed (5.6%) #> [20 | high] CH4: 2 removed (3.8%); CO2: 1 removed (1.9%) #> [20 | low] CH4: 0 removed (0%); CO2: 0 removed (0%) #> [30 | high] CH4: 2 removed (3.7%); CO2: 2 removed (3.7%) #> [30 | low] CH4: 1 removed (1.8%); CO2: 1 removed (1.8%) #> [40 | high] CH4: 1 removed (1.9%); CO2: 2 removed (3.7%) #> [40 | low] CH4: 0 removed (0%); CO2: 3 removed (5.6%) #> [50 | high] CH4: 0 removed (0%); CO2: 1 removed (1.9%) #> [50 | low] CH4: 0 removed (0%); CO2: 2 removed (3.6%) #> [60 | high] CH4: 0 removed (0%); CO2: 1 removed (1.8%) #> [60 | low] CH4: 1 removed (1.8%); CO2: 1 removed (1.8%) #> [70 | high] CH4: 0 removed (0%); CO2: 1 removed (1.8%) #> [70 | low] CH4: 1 removed (1.8%); CO2: 1 removed (1.8%) #> [80 | high] CH4: 1 removed (1.8%); CO2: 2 removed (3.6%) #> [80 | low] CH4: 1 removed (1.9%); CO2: 2 removed (3.7%) #> [90 | high] CH4: 0 removed (0%); CO2: 2 removed (3.8%) #> [90 | low] CH4: 1 removed (1.9%); CO2: 1 removed (1.9%)

title: Example flux processing
text: Let’s run plot_calculated_fluxes() function quality controlled fluxes make sure everything looks right.
code: plot_calculated_fluxes(   genx_fluxes_qaqced |>     # Plotting function requires TIMESTAMP column     dplyr::rename(TIMESTAMP = flux_time) ) plot_calculated_fluxes(   chapada_fluxes_qaqced |>     dplyr::rename(TIMESTAMP = flux_time),   group_cols = "location" )

title: autochamber
text: Utilities processing greenhouse gas data automated chambers R. Contact: Abigail Lewis (abigail.sl.lewis@gmail.com)

title: autochamber
text: autochamber provides functions process CH₄, CO₂, N₂O data automated flux chambers. includes tools : Visualizing, trimming, compiling raw data Calculating fluxes Flagging data using maintenance logs QA/QC filters Plotting calculated fluxes

title: autochamber
text: Full workflow vignette: https://abbylewis.github.io/autochamber/articles/Example_flux_processing.html

text: Calculate linear rate change CO2, CH4, /N2O flux interval

title: calculate_flux — calculate_flux
text: data_small dataset. Must contain columns Chamber, TIMESTAMP, CH4d_ppm/CO2d_ppm/N2Od_ppm cutoff_start Amount time remove start flux interval (seconds) cutoff_end End flux, determined start flux interval (seconds) group_cols Optional grouping variable. See details

title: calculate_flux — calculate_flux
text: Dataframe calculated fluxes

title: calculate_flux — calculate_flux
text: function calculates linear rate change gas (CO2, CH4, /N2O) concentrations. function requires dataframe input data contains columns gas concentrations (ppm), timestamps, column chamber index. default, fluxes grouped timestamp chamber index, additional grouping columns can identified group_cols (e.g., data multiple gas analyzers combined data_small)
